CANSLIM Scoring Methodology
Based on William O'Neil's CANSLIM framework — scores derived from available factsheet data.
Individual Criteria
Key Metric How it's calculated Grade thresholds
C
Current EPS
Quarterly earnings acceleration
 Most recent quarter EPS vs the same quarter one year prior (e.g. Q4 2025 vs Q4 2024). O'Neil requires ≥25% growth to qualify. A ≥25%   B ≥15%   C ≥5%   D ≥0%   F <0%
A
Annual EPS Growth
Multi-year earnings trend
 Average of the last 3 annual EPS year-over-year % changes. O'Neil looks for consistent ≥25% annual growth over several years. A ≥25%   B ≥15%   C ≥5%   D ≥0%   F <0%
S
Sales Growth
Revenue acceleration
 Most recent quarter revenue vs the same quarter one year prior. O'Neil wants recent quarterly sales up ≥25% to confirm earnings aren't from cost-cutting alone. A ≥25%   B ≥15%   C ≥5%   D ≥0%   F <0%
ROE
Return on Equity
Management quality proxy
 Most recent annual ROE. O'Neil's minimum quality threshold is 17%. Higher ROE indicates efficient use of shareholder capital. A ≥25%   B ≥17%   C ≥10%   D ≥0%   F <0%
NPM
Margin Trend
Profitability direction
 Net profit margin change over the last 3 annual data points (most recent minus 3 years ago, in percentage points). Expanding margins signal pricing power and operating leverage. A >+3pp   B >+1pp   C ≥−1pp   D ≥−3pp   F <−3pp
Accel
EPS Acceleration
Growth momentum
 Difference between the most recent quarter's EPS same-quarter YoY % and the prior quarter's same-quarter YoY %. Positive = growth rate is speeding up, a key O'Neil signal. A >+10pp   B ≥0pp   C ≥−5pp   D ≥−15pp   F <−15pp
Overall O'Neil Score (out of 10)
Numeric score: Each of the 6 criteria is converted to a number — A=4, B=3, C=2, D=1, F=0. The sum is divided by the maximum possible (6 × 4 = 24) and scaled to 10.
Score = (sum of all grade points) / 24 × 10

Letter grade: Calculated from the primary four criteria only (C, A, S, ROE) — these are the most directly supported by O'Neil's framework. Their average grade point maps to:
≥3.5 → A   ≥2.5 → B   ≥1.5 → C   ≥0.5 → D   <0.5 → F
What's not included
O'Neil's full CANSLIM framework also includes N (New products / 52-week highs), I (Institutional sponsorship), and M (Market direction). These require real-time market data and institutional holding data not available in the factsheet screenshots, so they are excluded from this scoring model. The L (Leader vs Laggard) criterion requires Relative Strength ranking against peers, also not available here.
